collision_resolution_records.description: Instagram optimal aspect ratio conflict: 4:5 (1080x1350) vs 3:4 (1080x1440)
collision_resolution_records.thesis: 4:5 (1080x1350) is the longstanding standard and widely documented.
collision_resolution_records.antithesis: Late 2025/2026 Instagram feed shifted to preferring 3:4 (1080x1440) for maximum vertical coverage.
collision_resolution_records.resolution: Paraconsistent synthesis: TCGE agent defaults to 1080x1350 (4:5) for maximum cross-platform compatibility (IG + cross-post workflows), but exposes an `aspect_ratio_override` flag in carousel_payload_v1.json for operators who confirm IG-only distribution targeting 3:4.

collision_resolution_records.description: TikTok file size spec conflict: 100KB suggested vs 1920x1080 maximum resolution (implies >100KB at high quality)
collision_resolution_records.thesis: 100KB is stated as the suggested maximum for TikTok carousel images.
collision_resolution_records.antithesis: At 720x1280px PNG with meaningful visual complexity, 100KB produces visible compression artifacts, harming the aesthetic fingerprint.
collision_resolution_records.resolution: The TCGE agent targets 100KB as a platform-optimized ceiling via WebP conversion with quality=88 as the default; the Spatial Canvas Calculator applies SSIM-guided compression to ensure visual fidelity before upload. The carousel_payload_v1.json stores both `raw_asset_path` and `compressed_asset_path`.

collision_resolution_records.description: Creative freedom vs schema determinism in JSON generation
collision_resolution_records.thesis: Forcing JSON output simultaneously with ideation causes a Projection Tax, degrading semantic reasoning.
collision_resolution_records.antithesis: Free-form drafts without schema enforcement produce API-breaking JSON hallucinations.
collision_resolution_records.resolution: DCCD (arXiv:2603.03305) resolves this via bifurcated inference. Phase 1 (THINK): unconstrained semantic draft; Phase 2 (COMPILE): draft-conditioned DFA-masked constrained decoding. This eliminates both failure modes simultaneously.
